subject: office-practice
question: Miscellaneous file should be opened when

options.A: the original file form document cannot be located
options.B: there is insufficient correspondence from one source to justify opening new file.
options.C: there is insufficient time for the filing clerk to assemble relevant document to open a new file
options.D: there are sufficient documents from various sources to justify opening a new file

explanation: Miscellaneous file should be opened when there is insufficient time for the filing clerk to assemble relevant document to open a new file.

subject: office-practice
question: Transmission of information between officers of the same level is
explanation: Horizontal communication is used in the transmission of information between officers of the same level. It is used to disseminate information or messages between officers at the same level.

subject: office-practice
question: "Who is who" is a book that contains information of

options.A: important people who are still living.

explanation: "Who is who" is a book that contains information of important people who are still living. It is a book that contains the name of prominent people.

subject: office-practice
question: Which of the following documents is handled by the receptionist?
explanation: Telephone message pad is one of the documents handled by the receptionist.

subject: office-practice
question: The type of incoming mail that should be delivered to their addresses unopened is
explanation: Registered mail is a type of incoming mail that should be delivered to their addresses unopened. It is a type of mail that is sealed.

subject: office-practice
question: Filing document according to dates is
explanation: Chronological filing is a filing method whereby documents are filed according to date.

subject: office-practice
question: The three components of a short report are the

options.C: terms of reference, body and conclusion.
options.D: terms of reference, body and recommendations

explanation: A short report comprises of introduction, body and conclusion.

subject: office-practice
question: The communication channel in the diagram above is
explanation: The communication channel in the diagram below is lateral communication channel General manager ↓             ↓ Personal manager   marketing manager

subject: office-practice
question: A committee which ceases to exist after completing its assignment is
explanation: An investigatory committee is a committee which ceases to exist after completing its assignment. It is setup to make findings about a particular matter or issue in an organisation.

subject: office-practice
question: Which of the following statements is true about a quotation? It shows the

options.B: quality of goods in the warehouse.

explanation: Quotation is the supplier's reply to the letter of enquiry which states the price, terms of payment, delivery dates and any discount. It is a statement.

subject: office-practice
question: To break a tie resulting from a poll, the chairman should

options.B: use his power of casting vote
options.C: use his discretion to take a decision
options.D: ask his executive members to advise him

explanation: To break a tie resulting from a poll, the chairman should use his power of casting vote.

subject: office-practice
question: The raw materials and equipment needed in an organization should be bought by the
explanation: Purchasing department is the department that is responsible for buying raw materials, stationery, component, trolls, plant and machinery e.t.c

subject: office-practice
question: A letter sent by Mrs. Benson to her husband by post through his office address is an example of
explanation: Personal Mail: These are mails addressed to particular individuals. They are normally open to whom they are addressed.

subject: office-practice
question: In order to eliminate barrier to effective communication, the sender should

options.A: use as much jargons as he can.
options.B: consider the level of recipient's education.
options.C: expect the recipient to use a dictionary in decoding.
options.D: discourage the recipient from giving a feedback.

explanation: Consider the level of recipient's education in order to eliminate barrier to effective communication.

subject: office-practice
question: The machine that is used to destroy unwanted papers into unreadable pieces is
explanation: Shredding machine is a machine that cuts document in to small pieces so that no one can read them. Unwanted documents are fed into the machine and shredded into strips.

subject: office-practice
question: A diagram that shows the line of responsibilities and authorities in a company is
explanation: Organizational chart is a graphic representation or a blueprint of all units, divisions and departments and their duties in an organisation.

subject: office-practice
question: A meeting conducted yearly by companies is
explanation: Annual general meeting: This is a compulsory meeting which all companies are required by law to hold once in a year. The law stipulates that the gap between two such meetings should not exceed fifteen months. It is conducted yearly by the companies.

subject: office-practice
question: The department that is responsible for replenishing stock in an organization is the
explanation: Purchasing department: This is the department that is responsible for buying raw materials, stationery, components, tools, plant & machinery e.t.c in an organisation. It is also responsible for replenishing stock in an organisation.

subject: office-practice
question: An advantage of central filing is that it ensures
explanation: The merit or advantage of central filing is that it ensures easy or quick access to files.

subject: office-practice
question: Mails requiring prompt attention are
explanation: Urgent mails: These are mails that require prompt attention. Once a mail is marked 'urgent', it should be attended to first before others. The contents should be checked immediately to facilitate speedy action.

subject: office-practice
question: Which of the following equipment is used for electronic filing?
explanation: Computer is an electronic device that accept data through input unit, process the data according to the instruction supplied to it by the user and transfer the result to a unit where it can be stored or print out. It is an equipment or device that is used for electronic filing.

subject: office-practice
question: The correspondence conveying the same information to different organization is/are
explanation: Circulars is correspondence conveying the same information to different organisation. It is an official letter that is sent to a large number of people at the same time.

subject: office-practice
question: The list of items to be discussed in a meeting is
explanation: Agenda is a list of all the items that have to be discussed at a meeting. It is arranged in the order in which they will be discussed.

subject: office-practice
question: Which of the following factors will be considered before buying office furniture?
explanation: Size of the furniture is one of the factors that will be considered before buying office furniture.

subject: office-practice
question: The principles of organization which states that a worker should not take orders from more than one superior officer is
explanation: Span of control: is the principles of organisation which states that a worker should not take order from more than one superior officer. It states tha number of subordinate that a superior officer can take.

subject: office-practice
question: One of the ways a receptionist show pleasantries to visitors is by

options.B: welcoming all visitors with a smile
options.C: welcoming them with a big hug

explanation: One of the ways a receptionist shows pleasantries to visitors is by welcoming all visitors with a smile.

subject: office-practice
question: One of the advantages of a close office layout is that it

options.A: enhances the prestige of the user.
options.D: enables common use of office equipment.

explanation: One of the advantages of a close office layout is that it enhances the prestige of the user.

subject: office-practice
question: Particulars of letters sent out from an office are entered in the
explanation: Dispatch book is a document that shows where the particulars of letters sent out from an office is documented.

subject: office-practice
question: A requirement of a valid meeting is that

options.A: some proxies should be in attendance.
options.C: all members should be in attendance.
options.D: items of its agenda should be completed.

explanation: A requirement of a valid meeting is that there should be a quorum. A quorum is the minimum number of members of a meeting that must be present before the commencement of a meeting.

subject: office-practice
question: A filing system where records are kept by various section within an organization is
explanation: Departmental filing system is a filing system where records are kept by various section within an organisation. It is different from centralized because all the departments create and maintain their files separately.

subject: office-practice
question: The term office equipment refers to
explanation: Office equipment refers to tools or machines that are used to carry out particular jobs or activities. They are assets used for the day-to-day running of an office.

subject: office-practice
question: Which of the following document is not used for business transaction?
explanation: Circular is not used for business transaction. It is an official letter that is sent to different number of people at the same time.

subject: office-practice
question: The opinion of an investigatory committee on its findings is stated in the report as
explanation: Recommendation is the opinion of an investigatory committee on its findings. It is the report of an Investigating committee on the issuers or findings.

subject: office-practice
question: One of the disadvantages of numerical system of filing is that

options.B: there may be congestion in a file
options.D: there is difficulty of document retrieval where date is unknown

explanation: One of the disadvantages of numerical system of filing is that there is difficulty of document retrieval where date is unknown.

subject: office-practice
question: The abbreviation "ref" on the letterhead of an organization represents
explanation: The abbreviation "ref" on the letterhead of an organisation represents reference.

subject: office-practice
question: A filing classification in which documents are stored according to their title is
explanation: Subject filing is a filing classification in which documents are stored or filed according to their title or topic. The topic or title of the document forms that basis for this system.

subject: office-practice
question: A good information should be
explanation: A good information should be relevant, clear, accurate, timeliness, target, quality and complete.

subject: office-practice
question: Economy in the use of office equipment is one of the advantages of
explanation: One of the advantages of an open office is economy. This is because the cost of lighting and heating is reduced and also there is saving on floor space simply because the office is not partitioned.

subject: office-practice
question: Formal meeting held by the directors of an organization to consider policy issues is
explanation: Board meeting is a formal meeting held by the board of directors of an organisation to consider policy issues.

subject: office-practice
question: Which of the following types of correspondence is used to convey information to a large number of customers?
explanation: Circular letters is a type of correspondence that is used to convey information to a large number of customers.

subject: office-practice
question: The part of a report where the highlights of an investigatory committee's assignment are stated is the
explanation: Body is the part of a report where the highlights of an investigating committee's assignment or findings are stated.

subject: office-practice
question: An example of open office in a school is the
explanation: Staff room is an example of open office in a school system.

subject: office-practice
question: One of the duties of a mail room clerk is to

options.B: ensure that enclosures are attached to outgoing mail.
options.C: give advance notice to addresses of incoming mail.
options.D: return uncleared letters to the writers.

explanation: One of the duties of a mail clerk is to ensure that enclosures are attached to outgoing mail.

subject: office-practice
question: Standardized letters which give the same information and reply to regular inquires and requests are
explanation: Standardized letters which give the same Information and reply to regular inquires and requests are confidential letters.

subject: office-practice
question: The use of a picture to convey information to receiver is an example of
explanation: Visual communications is communication through visual aid. It is the conveyance of ideas and details in form of maps, photography, signs, drawings, graphs, charts, pictures and video.

subject: office-practice
question: The process of interpreting a received message is
explanation: Decoding is a process of interpreting a received message

subject: office-practice
question: The means of internal communication in an organization is
explanation: Circular is the means of internal communication in an organisation. It is used to pass across information to all workers in an organisation.

subject: office-practice
question: A document from the seller to the buyer showing the date, description and quantity of goods supplies is
explanation: Delivery note is a document sent by the seller to the buyer for signature when goods delivered to him and it will serve as evidence that delivery has been made. It shoes description of goods, quantity of goods and date of supply.

subject: office-practice
question: In an organization, a receptionist may sometimes act as
explanation: In an organisation, a receptionist may sometimes act as secretary.

subject: office-practice
question: The tenure of an ad-hoc committee is
explanation: Ad-hoc committee is setup to organise a special assignment and their tenure is six month only.

subject: office-practice
question:
Read the case below carefully and answer this question PROBLEMS CAUSED BY THE MANAGING DIRECTOR OF OKF LIMITED OKF limited, an initially fast growing company, was established twelve years ago to deal in office machines. The vision of the Managing Director, Dr. Rock Bansco was that the Company would become the leading supplier of office machines. He also hoped that the company would provide employment for many secondary school leavers. Within the first five years of the company's inception, its profit grew rapidly. The customers increased so much that by the sixth year of the company's operation, the staff strength had become more than one hundred. However, all the workers who were employed in its sixth year of operation were both unqualified and inexperienced, worse still, they were given supervisory positions because of their relationship to the Managing Director in one way or the other. As a large company, OKF Limited had six Heads of Department. However, the Managing Director hardly involved any of them in the day-to-day running of the company. He did all recruitment, purchases as well as salaries and wages administration. Staff salaries and wages were also very poor. As time went on, virtually all the staff became unhappy and this affected their morale. The Managing Director recently called an emergency meeting to address the staff, surprisingly, he did not appoint anyone to record the proceedings of the meeting.
a) State two problems in OKF Limited.
b) Outline departments in OKF Limited that should be responsible for the following activities:
c) i) When is an emergency meeting called by the management of an organisation?
ii) What is the meeting's related title of the officer who should have recorded the meeting proceedings?
iii) State two duties the officer in (c)(ii) should have carried out before the day of the meeting
iv) List five headings under which the officer would have recorded the proceedings of the meeting.
explanation:
a) - Inexperience workers or staff. - Lack of delegation of duties.
b) - Recruitment of workers is done by personnel department. - Sales promotion is carried out by sales and marketing department - Acquisition of machines: Purchasing department - Payments: Finance department/Account department.
c) i) When there is urgent need to discuss and act prompt on some issues affecting the organisation. ii) The Secretary is the officer who should have recorded the meeting proceedings. iii) - He prepared the agenda with the help of the chairman. - He prepares and sends the notice of meeting to members. - He circulates documents needed for the meeting. - He receives apologies from members who are not able to attend the meeting. - He prepares the chairman's agenda.
iv) - Heading - Participants - Approval of previous minutes - Action items - Announcement - Next meeting - Adjournment - Signature line

subject: office-practice
question:
a) i) Explain this office document: Delivery note. ii) Explain this office document: Receipt. iii) Explain this office document: Bin card. iv) Explain this office document: Quotation.
b) Identify the types of equipment which would be used to perform this task in an office;
i) attaching a cheque to a letter to be sent to suppliers ii) boring holes on letters received in the registry for filing. iii) getting a carbon copy of a query letter to a worker. iv) sending an exact copy of a document to an overseas partner. v) making more copies of minutes of a meeting. vi) disposing of unwanted documents.
c) Explain this type of mail:
i) Personal mail ii) Confidential mail iii) Urgent mail
explanation:
a) i)Delivery note: This is a document sent by the seller to the buyer for signature when goods are delivered to him and it will serve as evidence that delivery has been made. It is used when goods are transported by the seller's means of transportation ii) Receipt: This is an acknowledgment of money received from the buyer by the seller. It is a document which confirms that payment has been made. It is prepared by the seller and sent to the buyer stating the total amount received. It is an evidence of payment. iii) Bin Card: This shows the record of receipts and issues of materials in the store. It is kept by the store keeper to record entries balance of stock at a particular time. iv) Quotation: This is the supplier's reply to the letter of enquiry which states the price, terms of payment, delivery dates and any discount. It is a statement of price given to a buyer when he asks his supplier for quotation.
b) i) Stapling machine ii) Perforating machine iii) Carbon paper iv) Fax machine v) Photocopy machine vi) Shredding machine
c) i) Personal mails: These are mails addressed to particular individual. They are normally opened by the person to whom they are addressed. ii) Confidential mails: These are mails shared with only a few people for furthering certain purposes, it is a mail that people should not have access to . It is always sealed and stamped. iii) Urgent mail: This type of mail require prompt attention. Once a mail is marked 'urgent', it should be attended to first before others. The contents should be checked immediately to facilitate speedy action.

subject: office-practice
question:
a) i) What us information ? ii) List four uses of information in an office?
b) State five importance of a report.
c) What is the difference between a statutory report and a routine report ?
explanation:
a) i) This consists of data which has been recorded, organised, classified and processed into useful and meaningful form. It can be viewed as already processed data into the form which is meaningful to the recipient in order to enable them take decisions or actions
ii) - Achievement of organizational objectives - Improvement of customer relation. - Increased profit. - Improvement on working environment. - Accurate forecasting - Useful for planning and decision making
b) - Basis for decision making - Provision of concrete evidence - Enhances an organisation's image - Increases the probability of success in an organisation - Availability of valuable conclusions and suggestions - Provisions of information on diverse are of human endeavour - Provision of technical information on specialised fields.
c) Statutory report: This is a report that a company or organisation must make by law. It is mandatory submission of financial and non-financial information to a government agency while Routine report is report written for management about a project at some specified time. It is written periodically e.g daily, weekly e.t.c

subject: office-practice
question:
a) As a Secretary of the Secretarial Students Association, write a notice of meeting to invite members to a meeting to be held in three weeks time.
b) Explain this media of communication:
i) Print ii) Electronic
c) Identify and explain four attributes of good filing system.
explanation:
Specimen 1 Queens College Sabo-Yaba road. Lagos. Notice of meeting A meeting of Secretarial Students Association is hereby given that the 4th general meeting will take place at the school premises by 3pm on thursday 30th Nov., 2018 to discuss the following issues; (i) End of the year party of the association. (ii) donations regarding building of structure in the school by members (iii) deliberation on how much to contribute by each member. A.A Lawal Secretary. Specimen 2 Queens College Sabo-yaba Road. Lagos Notice of meeting to members Date: 30th Nov., 2018. From: Secretary To: All members Subject: Annual end of the year party and building of structure in the school. References: 16/10 A meeting of Secretarial Students Association for the month of November will take place on the 30th of November at the school auditorium. Thank you. A.A Lawal Secretary.
b) i) Print media is the printed version of telling the news, primarily through newspapers and magazines. Before the invention and widespread use of printing presses, printed materials had to be written by hand.
ii) Electronic media is media that uses electromechanical device to access the content broadcast or storage media that take advantage of electronic technology. They may include television, radio, internet, fax, C.D-ROMs, DVD and other medium that requires electricity or digital encoding of information.
c) - Security: A good filing system should ensure safety and security of all documents. - Accessibility: The filing system should ensure that documents are easily accessible by users when they are needed. - Simplicity: A good filing system should be simple to understand and operate. It must not be too difficult to operate. - Economical: The best system should be the most economical to the organisation. Filing system should be economical to install and operate.

subject: office-practice
question:
a) State five functions of Finance Department in an organization.
b) State five importance of communication in an organization
explanation:
a) - Keeping of financial record of the organisation. - Controlling of expenditure of the organisation. - Dealing with tax matters of the organisation. - Managing of financial resources of the organisation. - Preparing of personnel cost of the organisation. - Recording of organisation's revenue. - Auditing records of the organisation.
b) - Quick dissemination of Information. - Facilities payment for goods and services. - Advertisement of goods and services. - Facilitates international or foreign trade. - Risk and Cost reduction. - Ensuring close contact between business men - Improvement of trade. - It ensures quick delivery of documents.

subject: office-practice
question:
a) Differentiate between adjournment and postponement .
b) i) What is office layout ? ii) State three benefits of open office layout. iii) State four factors to be considered before locating an office.
explanation:
a) Adjournment: This means to hold a meeting over until a later date. When the motion for an adjournment is moved, all deliberation stops while postponement means when a meeting is shifted from a particular date to another. It is the act of putting off a meeting to another date.
b) i) It is the practice of calculating the required office space and detailing the use of the total area available in order to ensure the practical arrangement of furniture and equipment for proper utilization of space. It is the same as office set-up
ii) - It increases staff morale - Economy in the use of space - Free flow of work - Cost minimization - increasing productivity.
iii) - Cost of construction - Accessibility by workers and customers - Security - Space - Customer's convenience - Possibility of future expansion.

subject: office-practice
question: As the purchasing officer of a service provider firm planning to purchase office equipment, state four factors you would consider in purchasing the equipment.
explanation: - Space to accommodate the equipment - Training requirement - Staff support - Lifespan of the equipment - Cost of acquisition and installation - Availability of spare parts - Investment options.

subject: office-practice
question:
OVERCOMING THE CHALLENGES AT THEOLAS INTERNATIONAL COMPANY Prior to Mr.Okonkwo's employment as the Human Resource Manager of Theolas International Company, the organization was faced with the problem of absenteeism by its workers. They came late to work and left before the closing time. As a result of this, the volume of production of the company reduced. This inturn, reduced the income of the organization so much that payments of workers' wages and salaries became difficult. Many experienced workers began to leave the firm for greener pastures. The firm also found it difficult to meet its financial obligations to suppliers. On assumption of duty, Mr. Okonkwo quickly swung into action. He carried out an investigation to ascertain the causes of these problems. One of his findings was that workers bitterly complained about their conditions of service. Although their wages and salaries were acceptable, they asked for other benefits from the company which were denied them Mr. Okonkwo also discovered that workers were employed without following due process. Finally, he found out that no measure was put in place to regulate the punctuality of workers. Armed with these finding, Mr Okonkwo addressed each of the issues and put in place adequate measures to ensure proper supervision and satisfaction of workers.
(a). Suggest one measure that Mr. Okonkwo could use to improve staff regularity at work
(b).  Apart from irregularity at work, state two other problems in Theolas International Company
(c). List six welfare packages that would improve workers satisfaction at Theolas International Company
(d). State four pre-employment activities the company should have carried out in recruiting its staff
explanation:
(a). Mr Okonkwo should put up a measure that would regulate the punctuality of workers after the company fulfills its own obligation.
(b). - The volume of production of the company reduced which led to the reduction of the organisation's income which as well made it difficult to pay worker's salaries and wages. - The firm also found it difficult to meet its financial obligations to suppliers.
(c).
- Reward the workers - Recognize the workers - Training and investment - Compensation - Drive communication and transparency - Promote good health - Allow employees to shape their own roles - Correct planning of employee's schedules
(d). - identify the hring need - Devise a recruitment plan - Write a job description - Adertise the position - Recruit the position - Review applications - Screening - Interviews
